prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins with a completely black frame; no objects, people, text, or setting are visible.

By the final sampled frame, the image has changed to a black-and-white product shot. A rectangular bottle is shown on the left side of the frame, likely a mouthwash or oral antiseptic product. Its label reads “MICRIN” in large letters, with smaller text above and below that is partly legible; the smaller text appears to include “ORAL ANTISEPTIC” and “MOUTH WASH & GARGLE.” The bottle has a cap at the top and a small oval emblem near the lower front.

On the right side of the frame, large bold text reads: “Stop bad breath germs.” The background is plain and softly shaded, keeping attention on the product and the written message. A small circular mark or logo appears in the lower-right corner, but its details are not clear.
